name: Lanikai Pillbox
tagline: Two WWII bunkers over the Mokulua Islands — the most photogenic sunrise hike on the windward side.
parking: Street parking on Kaelepulu Dr — arrive before sunrise to find a spot
hours: Sunrise to sunset (no gates; unofficial trail on public land)
bestTime: Sunrise — start 30 min before for the climb; the light over the Mokes is unbeatable
talkStory: Officially the Ka'iwa Ridge Trail, these concrete bunkers were built after Pearl Harbor as coastal defense against Japanese invasion — soldiers watched for enemy ships from these exact vantage points. The name Lanikai ('heavenly sea') was coined in the 1920s when the area was subdivided. The Mokulua Islands offshore are state seabird sanctuaries. Start 30 minutes before sunrise for the famous golden light that makes this Oahu's most photographed hike.
